caseStudy.title: Intelligent Document Processing
caseStudy.description: Automating extraction from unstructured legal and financial documents.
caseStudy.overview: A legal services firm processed thousands of contracts, filings, and correspondence monthly, with teams of paralegals manually extracting key clauses, dates, and obligations. The goal was to automate extraction to allow legal professionals to focus on higher-value advisory work.
caseStudy.problem: Manual document review was the primary bottleneck in the firm's workflow, with each contract requiring 2–4 hours of paralegal time. Error rates in extraction were estimated at 3–5%, creating downstream risks in contract management. High paralegal turnover added ongoing training costs.
caseStudy.solution: A document intelligence pipeline was built using large language models fine-tuned for legal document understanding. Extracted entities and clauses were structured into a review interface that presented results alongside the source document for human verification. A confidence scoring system flagged low-certainty extractions for priority review.

results.summary: Paralegals now review AI-extracted results rather than performing manual extraction, shifting their work to verification and exception handling. The firm has taken on 40% more client volume without adding headcount.
